political.s: Potbelly has minimal federal political activity. Limited campaign expenditure data available from 2012 showing $14,272 in vendor payments. No significant PAC or lobbying activity documented.

charity.s: Potbelly maintains corporate donation and sponsorship programs including in-shop fundraisers and employee volunteer initiatives. Company supports youth employment programs and community organizations through direct giving and promotional activities.
charity.focus: Youth employment, local community organizations, education

environment.s: Potbelly has implemented green initiatives including LED lighting, low-flow water fixtures, EPA-designated cleaning products, and low-VOC paints in new stores. Renovation practices emphasize reusing existing building materials when possible.

labor.s: In May 2023, a Portland location paid consulting firm $100,000 to reduce union organizing efforts and allegedly fired three workers leading the campaign. Company has also acknowledged potential wage pressure from minimum wage increases and exposure to employment-related lawsuits.

dei.s: Potbelly acknowledges diversity in marketing and customer outreach but has not publicly disclosed comprehensive DEI initiatives or targets. Company mentions inclusive leadership in messaging but lacks published DEI commitments or metrics.

animals.s: Not applicable - food service business focused on sandwiches and prepared foods. No animal testing or cruelty practices relevant to core business operations.

guns.s: Not applicable - quick-service restaurant with no connection to firearms manufacturing, sales, or distribution.

privacy.s: Potbelly maintains privacy policies for customer data through its loyalty program (Potbelly Perks) and website. Company offers opt-out options for data sharing and maintains standards for personal information handling, though comprehensive third-party data practices not fully detailed.

execPay.s: CEO Robert Wright earned $3.0M in 2024 total compensation (25% salary, 75% bonus/equity), near industry median for comparable-sized hospitality companies. CEO owns 2.3% of company shares, indicating alignment with shareholder interests.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
